summary: Intel has formally discontinued its Quantum Passes open-source project, which since 2023 provided supplementary compiler passes for the LLVM-based Intel Quantum Software Development Kit. The Quantum Passes repository, last updated alongside Intel Quantum Software Development Kit 1.1.1 over a year ago, is now archived and read-only, with Intel stating it will no longer maintain or accept patches for the project. While there has been no new release of the Intel Quantum Software Development Kit in the past year, Intel has not formally discontinued the larger kit. This move reflects a wider pattern of Intel scaling back several open-source initiatives amid cost-cutting, despite the ongoing interest in quantum computing development tools. (Source: Phoronix, Michael Larabel, 14 February 2026)
